factors.summary: Volume trends show mixed performance across different groups. While the Defense Groups are maintaining stable unit volumes through multi-year contracts, the Aerospace sector is experiencing a decline, particularly in large-cabin aircraft deliveries.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing mixed organic growth, with evidence of new contract awards indicating growth in certain defense groups, while overall revenue has shown a decrease in key sectors, suggesting a deceleration in organic growth.

evidence.text: Each of our defense groups received notable contract awards during the second quarter of 2016. Combat Systems awards included... Information Systems and Technology awards included... Marine Systems awards included...
Revenue was lower in the second quarter and first six months of 2016 driven primarily by decreased volume in our Aerospace and Combat Systems groups.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Decrease in overall revenue despite new contracts.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed across different groups. The Defense Groups show stable demand with a positive book-to-bill ratio, while the Aerospace group also reports stable demand with healthy order intake.

evidence.text: The book-to-bill ratio (orders divided by revenue) in our Information Systems and Technology group exceeded one-to-one in the second quarter of 2016, continuing a trend of book-to-bill in the group equal to or greater than one-to-one in eight of the last 10 quarters.
Demand in the Aerospace group continues to be healthy and we received orders for Gulfstream aircraft across our product portfolio in the second quarter of 2016.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Stable demand with positive book-to-bill ratios.

factors.summary: The backlog orders show a mixed trend across different groups. While the defense groups saw a slight decrease in total backlog, their potential contract value increased. On the other hand, the Aerospace group experienced a notable decline in backlog orders.

evidence.text: Total backlog in our defense groups was $51.5 billion on July 3, 2016 , down slightly from $52.1 billion on April 3, 2016 . Estimated potential contract value was $23.6 billion on July 3, 2016 , up over 6 percent compared with $22.1 billion on April 3, 2016 .
Aerospace funded backlog... ended the second quarter of 2016 with backlog of $11.8 billion compared with $12.6 billion on April 3, 2016.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed trends in backlog orders across different groups.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in the Aerospace group, which has seen improvements in operating margins due to cost savings and enhanced performance. Additionally, the Defense Groups are also experiencing a shift towards higher-margin areas, as evidenced by the book-to-bill ratio in the Information Systems and Technology group exceeding one-to-one.

evidence.text: The book-to-bill ratio (orders divided by revenue) in our Information Systems and Technology group exceeded one-to-one...

Operating margin in the Aerospace group increased 90 basis points in the second quarter and 60 basis points in the first six months of 2016, driven primarily by cost savings initiatives and improved operating performance.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shift towards higher-margin segments.

evidence.text: The funded portion of this backlog includes items that have been authorized and appropriated by the Congress...
The Combat Systems group’s full-year revenue to increase slightly in 2016 despite the foreign exchange headwind discussed above.

factors.summary: The contract backlog is showing mixed trends with slight decreases in total funded backlog and increases in unfunded backlog in some areas, while other sections indicate a slight overall decrease in total backlog and a focus on major ship programs.

evidence.text: "Total backlog in our defense groups was $51.5 billion on July 3, 2016 , down slightly from $52.1 billion on April 3, 2016 . Estimated potential contract value was $23.6 billion on July 3, 2016 , up over 6 percent compared with $22.1 billion on April 3, 2016 ."
"Total backlog... was $63.2 billion at the end of the second quarter of 2016, down 2.3 percent from $64.7 billion on April 3, 2016."
